title: Shelf fillers
description: Receive, store, and issue merchandise, materials, equipment, and other items from stockroom, warehouse, or storage yard to fill shelves, racks, tables, or customers' orders. May operate power equipment to fill orders. May mark prices on merchandise and set up sales displays.

tasks.task: Receive, store, and issue items from stockroom, warehouse, or storage areas. This includes obtaining merchandise from bins or shelves, issuing materials based on requisitions, and packing customer purchases. Stock shelves, racks, cases, bins, and tables with new or transferred merchandise, and store items in an orderly and accessible manner. Maintain records of orders, usage, and inventory levels. Examine stock for defects and report damage. Clean and maintain storage areas, supplies, tools, and equipment to ensure safety compliance, and assist or direct other workers as needed. Operate equipment such as forklifts and arrange display signage to promote sales. Answer customer questions and advise on merchandise selections.

Requisition merchandise from suppliers based on various factors such as customer demand. Transfer or transport packages to customers' vehicles when necessary. Check and confirm the accuracy of shipments by comparing invoices to received items. Handle pricing by marking stock items and computing item prices. Clean display cases, shelves, and aisles.
Take inventory and keep records related to outgoing orders and damaged stock. Recommend disposal of excess or obsolete stock and remove damaged items from the inventory.
Receive, unload, open, unpack, or issue sales floor merchandise and complete order receipts. Read orders to ascertain details about merchandise such as catalog numbers and quantities.
Pack and unpack items for stocking in warehouses, stockrooms, or storage yards.

Prepare documentation for contracts, transactions, or regulatory compliance.
Discuss goods or services information with customers or patrons.
Advise others on business or operational matters.
Distribute materials to employees or customers.
Monitor inventories of products or materials.
